findings.title: Full liquidation with protocol fees can leave the collateral bit permanently stuck on

findings.title: Reserve accounting trusts nominal transfer amounts instead of actual received amounts

best_agent_match.title: Transfers are accounted by requested amount instead of actual received amount

rejected_candidates.title: Non-collateral assets are automatically marked as collateral on first receipt
rejected_candidates.reason: The code does set the collateral bit for first-time receipt even when `liquidationThreshold == 0`, but those assets are ignored in account-data and liquidation calculations, and the holder can clear the flag while the balance exists. This is a state-integrity/UX issue, not a realistic protocol-level loss or lockup.

rejected_candidates.title: Liquidation Bonus of Zero Causes Permanent Liquidation Failure
rejected_candidates.reason: This requires a privileged, nonsensical reserve configuration. Governance/configurator can already brick a market with invalid parameters; the report does not show an unprivileged exploit or a bug reachable under normal reserve setup.

rejected_candidates.title: Integer Overflow in Isolation Mode Debt Calculation
rejected_candidates.reason: The addition is checked by Solidity 0.8 and the increment is narrowed with `toUint128()`, so overflow reverts rather than wraps. The configured isolation debt ceiling is also far below `uint128` range, so the claimed bypass is not feasible.

rejected_candidates.title: Price Oracle Manipulation Risk
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a generic single-oracle trust assumption, not a code-specific vulnerability in this codebase. No concrete oracle integration flaw or exploitable invariant break was identified.

rejected_candidates.title: Unchecked Callback in Flash Loan Enables Complex Reentrancy
rejected_candidates.reason: Flash-loan callbacks are intentional, and the implementation explicitly reorders state transitions to tolerate the callback. Without a concrete broken invariant or exploit path, generic reentrancy composability is not reportable.

rejected_candidates.title: Rounding Error in Liquidation Can Cause Dust Amounts
rejected_candidates.reason: This is ordinary integer rounding dust with no concrete path to meaningful fund loss, lockup, or protocol insolvency.

rejected_candidates.title: Division Before Multiplication Causes Precision Loss
rejected_candidates.reason: The cited arithmetic introduces only normal integer rounding and no demonstrated way to cross collateral or liquidation thresholds in a materially exploitable way.

rejected_candidates.title: Stable Debt Rebalance Rate Manipulation
rejected_candidates.reason: Permissionless stable-rate rebalancing is intended behavior and is gated by `validateRebalanceStableBorrowRate`. The report describes expected economic behavior, not a violation of protocol invariants.

rejected_candidates.title: Uninitialized Reserve Can Be Reinitialized
rejected_candidates.reason: `ReserveLogic.init` runs atomically and permanently sets `aTokenAddress`; there is no partial-initialization state that later permits a second init. The claim does not match the code.

rejected_candidates.title: Block Timestamp Dependency in Interest Accrual
rejected_candidates.reason: Use of `block.timestamp` for accrual is standard and the report does not provide a concrete exploit beyond negligible validator timestamp freedom.

rejected_candidates.title: No Access Control on rescueTokens Function
rejected_candidates.reason: `rescueTokens` is explicitly restricted by `onlyPoolAdmin`. A compromised admin draining funds is a governance/key-management trust assumption, not a contract vulnerability.
